[et_pb_section fb_built=”1″ fullwidth=”on” _builder_version=”4.11.3″ _module_preset=”default”][et_pb_fullwidth_slider _builder_version=”4.11.3″ _module_preset=”default” hover_enabled=”0″ sticky_enabled=”0″][et_pb_slide _builder_version=”4.11.3″ _module_preset=”default” heading=”Pause to help and lift another” alt=”Jesus sitting with a child” title_text=”jesus-with-a-child-full” background_image=”https://practice.pokealec.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/jesus-with-a-child-full.jpg” background_enable_image=”on” hover_enabled=”0″ sticky_enabled=”0″]

Savior, may I love my brother, as I know thou lovest me, find in thee my strength, my beacon, for thy servant I would be.

[/et_pb_slide][/et_pb_fullwidth_slider][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built=”1″ _builder_version=”4.11.3″ _module_preset=”default”][et_pb_row _builder_version=”4.11.3″ _module_preset=”default” column_structure=”1_3,2_3″][et_pb_column _builder_version=”4.11.3″ _module_preset=”default” type=”1_3″][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.11.3″ _module_preset=”default” hover_enabled=”0″ sticky_enabled=”0″ admin_label=”dark box” module_class=”dark_box”]

A more excellent way ~Howard W. Hunter

We need to walk more resolutely and more charitably the path that Jesus has shown. We need to “pause to help and lift another” and surely we will find “strength beyond [our] own.

Read More[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column _builder_version=”4.11.3″ _module_preset=”default” type=”2_3″][et_pb_slider _builder_version=”4.11.3″ _module_preset=”default”][et_pb_slide _builder_version=”4.11.3″ _module_preset=”default” hover_enabled=”0″ sticky_enabled=”0″ alt=”people doing service” title_text=”service-full” background_image=”https://practice.pokealec.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/service-full.jpg” background_enable_image=”on”]

The world in which we live would benefit greatly if men and women everywhere would exercise the pure love of Christ

[/et_pb_slide][/et_pb_slider][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built=”1″ _builder_version=”4.11.3″ _module_preset=”default”][et_pb_row _builder_version=”4.11.3″ _module_preset=”default” column_structure=”1_3,1_3,1_3″][et_pb_column _builder_version=”4.11.3″ _module_preset=”default” type=”1_3″][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.11.3″ _module_preset=”default” hover_enabled=”0″ sticky_enabled=”0″]

Bind Up Their Wounds ~Henry B. Eyring

I pray that we may prepare ourselves to give whatever priesthood service the Lord may set before us on our mortal journey.

Read more[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column _builder_version=”4.11.3″ _module_preset=”default” type=”1_3″][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.11.3″ _module_preset=”default” hover_enabled=”0″ sticky_enabled=”0″]

First Observe, Then Serve ~Linda K. Burton

With practice, each of us can become more like the Savior as we serve God’s children.

Read more[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column _builder_version=”4.11.3″ _module_preset=”default” type=”1_3″][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.11.3″ _module_preset=”default” hover_enabled=”0″ sticky_enabled=”0″]

Sisterhood: Oh, How We Need Each Other ~Bonnie L. Oscarson

We must stop concentrating on our differences and look for what we have in common.

Read more
[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section]
